articles.title: As heat wave looms, HP records above normal temperatures - The Times of India
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat") Himachal Pradesh
articles.full_text:
Shimla: Himachal Pradesh is witnessing unusually hot weather conditions with nine out of the 36 weather stations in the state recording temperatures above 30oC on Friday. Both the minimum and maximum continued to hover well above the normal across the state.
According to the Meteorological Centre, Shimla, Una recorded the highest maximum temperature of 34oC on Friday, followed by 33.6oC in Neri, Hamirpur.
The maximum temperature across the state remained above normal, ranging from 1 to 14oC above the average.
Mandi recorded a maximum of 33oC, which was 7.4o above normal; Kangra 32oC — 10.1o above normal; Sundernagar 32.8oC — 9.8o above normal; Bhuntar 31.8oC — 10.9o above normal; Solan 30.2oC — 8.1o above normal; and Dharamshala 30oC — 9.4o above normal.
High-altitude places like Kalpa and Keylong also witnessed a sharp rise in the mercury. While Kalpa recorded a maximum of 23.5oC — 14.3o above normal, Keylong recorded a day temperature of 14.2oC — 12.2o above normal.
Chamba recorded a maximum of 29.9oC, which was 4.5o above normal. At 25.3oC, Shimla was also warmer than usual. The maximum in the capital city was 10.1o above normal. Manali recorded a day temperature of 24.6oC — 10.2o above normal.
Also, for the first time since winter, the lowest minimum temperature jumped past the 0o mark on Friday, as Tabo in Lahaul Spiti recorded the lowest night temperature of 2.3oC.
Shimla recorded a low of 15.4o, which was 9.3o above normal. Minimum temperature in Manali was registered at 8.8oC — 7o above normal.
The meteorological department has predicted the possibility of light snow and rain in the high hills and a heatwave in the mid and low hills of Kullu, Mandi and Solan districts on March 7.
